qa_pairs.question: What is the default structure of an Apptainer SIF file and how is it typically created?
qa_pairs.answer: An Apptainer SIF (Singularity Image Format) file typically contains a read-only `squashfs` filesystem. It is usually produced by the `apptainer build` command, unless more advanced image-building methods are employed.

qa_pairs.question: Why is using an Apptainer SIF file considered the preferred option for container images?
qa_pairs.answer: SIF files are preferred because they result in a read-only image that is also much smaller due to compression. This design allows for very quick disk reads from the image.
